detaileddescription.para: Card in idle state during init
Any bit other than IDLE = error

briefdescription.para: Transfer one byte over SPI and return the received byte.

memberdef.argsstring: (const SYN_SD *sd, uint8_t cmd, uint32_t arg, uint8_t crc)
memberdef.name: sd_cmd

briefdescription.para: Send a 6-byte SD command and poll for R1 response.
Wait until the SD card is no longer busy (MISO = 0xFF).

simplesect.para: true if card became ready within timeout.

briefdescription.para: Read the CSD register and parse sector count.
Initialize the SD card over SPI.

detaileddescription.para: Performs power-up clocking, CMD0/CMD8/ACMD41 init, type detection via CMD58, and CSD parsing for capacity.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a caller-owned SYN_SD struct.
SPI bus index passed to syn_port_spi_*.

simplesect.para: SYN_OK on success, SYN_ERROR if no card or init failed.

briefdescription.para: Read one 512-byte sector from the SD card (CMD17).

parameterdescription.para: Output buffer — caller must provide at least 512 bytes.

simplesect.para: SYN_OK on success, SYN_ERROR on timeout or card error.

briefdescription.para: Write one 512-byte sector to the SD card (CMD24).

parameterdescription.para: Data to write — must be exactly 512 bytes.

simplesect.para: SYN_OK on success, SYN_ERROR on card rejection or timeout.

briefdescription.para: Flush the write pipeline — wait until card is idle (CMD13).

simplesect.para: SYN_OK if card is idle and error-free, SYN_ERROR otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Return total sector count parsed from the CSD register.

simplesect.para: Number of 512-byte sectors on the card.

briefdescription.para: SD card SPI block driver implementation.

detaileddescription.para:
Implements the SD simplified SPI spec:
CMD0 (GO_IDLE_STATE) reset to SPI mode
CMD8 (SEND_IF_COND) SDHC-capable detection
CMD9 (SEND_CSD) capacity via CSD v1/v2 parse
CMD13 (SEND_STATUS) sync / flush
CMD16 (SET_BLOCKLEN) SDSC: force 512-byte blocks
CMD17 (READ_SINGLE_BLOCK)
CMD24 (WRITE_BLOCK)
CMD55 (APP_CMD) ACMD prefix
CMD58 (READ_OCR) card type confirmation
ACMD41 (SD_SEND_OP_COND) card initialization
CRC: CMD0/CMD8 use hardcoded CRC7 constants. All other commands and data blocks use 0xFF dummy bytes (CRC mode off by default in SPI).
